title: Decimator (war machine)

{{Quote|The researchers refer to the weapon as the Decimator. It is sheathed in protective armor and seems to possess its own shielding. It also appears to be capable of targeting ''[sic]'' air as well as ground vehicles. Unfortunately, precious little else is known.|Bogeega Bu'daay|<ref name="CC" />}}
The '''Decimator''' was a war machine developed by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] at the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Equipped with [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and a long-range [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] that could target air and ground vehicles, the Decimator could engage and defeat craft as large as the [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]]. They were also able to ferry [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] around the battlefield, protecting them within the confines of the Decimator, though the vehicle itself was slow.

Developed at a secret testing facility located on [[Eredenn Prime]] and constructed at [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]] by [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], the Decimator was considered one of the most powerful combat vehicles of its era. However, before the Republic could use them, the Decimators were stolen by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Sev'rance Tann/Legends|Sev'rance Tann]]. Tann used them to [[Assault on Sarapin|capture]] the energy-producing [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Sarapin/Legends|Sarapin]], striking fear into the inhabitants of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] due to the ensuing blackouts. Her campaign provoked the Republic to send [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Echuu Shen-Jon]] to [[Hunt for the Decimator|hunt down]] Tann and her Decimators. Shen-Jon ultimately succeeded at the [[Battle of Krant]], and the Decimators saw no further action in the Clone Wars.

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|A land-based vehicle. Capable of tremendous destructive power.|Bogeega Bu'Daay, on the Decimator|<ref name="CC" />}}
The Decimator's body was roughly rectangular in shape, save for a tower that rose from the rear of the vehicle along its midline. Just in front of the tower, in the center of the vehicle, was a large [[Color/Legends|red]] orb that served a purpose in the firing mechanism. Even when not firing, energy circulated through the orb in [[Color/Legends|green]] waves. When the weapon was activated, the energy would coalesce, causing the orb to glow a brilliant green, before launching a single, focused green beam toward its target.<ref name="CC" />

Decimators were described by historians as having "giant [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannons" as their main weapons.<ref name="NEC" /> Each Decimator was comparable in size, [[Armor/Legends|armor]], and firepower to the largest [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] vehicles in service at the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], such as the [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]]. It was also described as possessing tremendous destructive power. Besides their firepower, Decimators also possessed the ability to transport up to ten [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] and were sheathed in thick layers of armor, as well as protective [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. However, for all their firepower and armor, Decimators were restricted to operating from land and could not float over [[Water/Legends|water]] or [[Magma/Legends|lava]], nor could they directly cross over cliffs or thick forests,<ref name="CC" /> despite possessing [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] for propulsion.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Role==
[[File:DecimatorBlackAndWhiteSWGB.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Decimator]]
With its powerful shields, turbolaser, and thick armor, the Decimator was suited to front-line combat. It could easily defeat comparable vehicles in combat, relying on its shields to absorb damage. The Decimator's weapon was limited to firing at just one target at a time and had to charge before it could fire again, which meant that getting off the first shot against comparable vehicles was not assured. The unique weapon of the Decimator could traverse the beam itself electronically though without need to traverse the emitter to hit aircraft as well, though it still had to charge between discharges.<ref name="CC" />

The slow speed of the Decimator and low firing rate meant that it was better equipped to combat other front-line assault vehicles rather than greater quantities of swifter adversaries. In particular, opponents that could close inside the firing arc of the Decimator's main weapon, such as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] or close-range assault [[Troop/Legends|troops]], were able to avoid its significant firepower. However, the ability to ferry ten additional infantry inside the Decimator meant that it could augment its defense by deploying these soldiers if the vehicle was threatened.<ref name="CC" />

==History==
{{Quote|We may find this new weapon useful for our cause.|Count Dooku, on the Decimator|<ref name="CC" />}}
The Decimator began as a weapons project by [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Testing of the weapons system was conducted in the [[Erediss system]] while the vehicles were actually manufactured on the Wookiee colony of [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]]. The first non-Republic-affiliated person to discover the Decimator's existence was [[Bogeega Bu'Daay]], an [[operative]] who informed the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] of the weapon's existence after infiltrating the Republic Decimator research center on [[Eredenn Prime]]. Bu'Daay passed his discovery on to [[Boorka]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who understood that the information would be fruitful if he gave it to someone who would be interested, namely the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, Bu'Daay was discovered by Wookiee personnel on Eredenn before he could divulge further information.<ref name="CC" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], one month after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]],<ref name="Timing">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the [[Hunt for the Decimator|Decimator Campaign]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], one month after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] and after the [[Dark Reaper campaign]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the Decimator Campaign shortly after the Battle of Geonosis, concurrent with the Dark Reaper campaign and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lok Durd/Legends|Lok Durd's]] [[Defense of Maridun/Legends|usage]] of the [[Defoliator/Legends|defoliator]]. A complete and updated [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] timeline has not been released by [[Lucasfilm]]</ref> after [[Battle of Kaer Orbital Platform|constructing]] a sizable mechanized [[Army/Legends|army]] on the [[Kaer Orbital Platform]] for use by the Confederacy, the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Sev'rance Tann/Legends|Sev'rance Tann]] journeyed to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Boorka, where the Hutt was willing to sell information about the Decimator project once Tann [[First Battle of Tatooine|destroyed]] a Republic [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]].<ref name="CC" />

Having learned from Boorka that the Republic was testing the Decimators on remote Eredenn, Tann [[Battle of Eredenn|raided]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. She attacked both [[Shard Test Site]] and [[Facet Test Site]], capturing their Decimators and several [[Decimator Facility|Decimator Facilities]] intact. However, she lacked the control codes to make them operational. Informed by Separatist [[Head of State/Legends|leader]] [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] that the codes were located in a nearby [[Eredenn Station|base]], Tann pushed on and seized a [[RA-7 protocol droid/Legends|data droid]] possessing the codes from the [[Decimator Laboratory]]. Despite this victory, she still needed to unlock the fail-safe mechanism programmed into the Decimators in order to use them. From the Decimator Laboratory, Tann also obtained the location of the Decimator manufacturing center on Alaris Prime and headed there next to decipher the codes necessary to use her new weapons.<ref name="CC" />

Tann landed on Alaris Prime and [[Battle of Alaris Prime (Hunt for the Decimator)|attacked]] a Republic Decimator facility surrounded by Wookiee outposts. Bringing the captured data [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to the facility, she unlocked the Decimators by having the droid interface with the main [[Computer/Legends|computer]], making the vehicles available to the Confederacy. Tann then destroyed the Decimator facilities on Alaris Prime and departed, ready to use the Decimators for the Confederacy.<ref name="CC" />

[[File:TannAndDecimatorsOnSarapinSWGB.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Sev'rance Tann/Legends|Sev'rance Tann]] and her Decimators on [[Sarapin/Legends|Sarapin]]]]
Tann used at least two of the vehicles to help her [[Assault on Sarapin|capture]] the Republic-controlled energy production world [[Sarapin/Legends|Sarapin]]. Having defeated the Republic defenders,<ref name="CC" /> Tann's Decimators also destroyed the geothermal generators. The loss of Sarapin's energy production facilities caused blackouts on numerous [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="NEC" /><ref name="TEA" /> The Separatists then established a new Decimator facility on [[Krant]] to continue production of the Decimators under Tann's supervision. To supply the facilities with raw materials, Tann employed a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Aereen]] that was under [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] purview.<ref name="CC" />

The facility on Aereen was soon [[Battle of Aereen|attacked]] and destroyed by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Echuu Shen-Jon]] just as Tann was inspecting a new contingent of Decimators on Krant. Shen-Jon had been tracking Tann and had already [[Recapture of Sarapin|reclaimed]] Sarapin; now he was intent on stopping her from deploying more Decimators. Shen-Jon took his Republic army to Krant and [[Battle of Krant|destroyed]] the [[Decimator Production Yards]] and the Decimators themselves, at least one of which was operational. Tann was [[Death/Legends|slain]] by Shen-Jon in a subsequent engagement, ending the use of the Decimators.<ref name="CC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Decimator first appeared in the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|computer game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' as the focus for both the Confederacy and Republic campaigns. The player starts with two of them available as hero units in the Confederacy's final mission on Sarapin and it is classified a Mech in-game. Its in-game stats are comparable to a heavy assault mech, with the addition of shielding, health regeneration and the ability to target aircraft.<ref name="CC" /> The Decimator and the campaign in which Sev'rance Tann employed them against the Republic were also mentioned in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. In the latest of these books, the Decimator is mistakenly referred to as the "Devastator." ''Essential Atlas'' co-author [[Daniel Wallace]] has stated that the use of the term "Devastator" was a mistake; the book should have said "Decimator."<ref name="Decimator email">{{EmailCite|email=Wookieepedia:Email/Decimator (war machine)|creator=[[Daniel Wallace]]|date=2010-06-15}}</ref>

Another point of confusion regarding the Decimator occurs in ''The New Essential Chronology'', where the Decimators are described as "tanks with giant turbolaser cannons mounted atop."<ref name="NEC" /> In ''Clone Campaigns'', Decimators only seem to have a single weapon,<ref name="CC" /> but it is possible that ''The New Essential Chronology'' implies they have multiple weapons,<ref name="NEC" /> as ''Clone Campaigns'' has game mechanics that are rarely consistent with the rest of [[canon]].<ref name="CC" />
